questions.q: On which tract of land did the Pandavas raise Indraprastha?
options: The banks of the Ganga at Kashi
questions.explanation: The Pandavas accepted the barren tract of Khandavaprastha as their half of the kingdom and transformed it into Indraprastha.

questions.q: Who granted the Pandavas the barren half of the kingdom where Indraprastha rose?
questions.explanation: King Dhritarashtra partitioned the realm and gave the Pandavas the wild Khandavaprastha tract.

questions.q: Whose celestial city was Indraprastha said to rival?
questions.explanation: The new capital grew so splendid it was compared to the heaven of Indra, whose name it bore.

questions.q: Who built the famed hall of illusions at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: Maya, the architect of the danavas, built the matchless assembly hall for Yudhishthira.

questions.q: Which imperial sacrifice did Yudhishthira perform at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira was consecrated emperor through the Rajasuya yajna held at Indraprastha.

questions.q: Who fell into a pool of water he mistook for a crystal floor at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana, deceived by Maya's illusions, fell fully clothed into a real pool he took for solid crystal.

questions.q: With which modern city is ancient Indraprastha traditionally identified?
questions.explanation: Tradition places Indraprastha at the site of present-day Delhi.

questions.q: On the banks of which river did Indraprastha stand?
questions.explanation: Indraprastha rose beside the Yamuna, on the lands cleared near the old Khandava forest.

questions.q: Who reigned from Indraprastha at the height of its glory?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira ruled from Indraprastha and was crowned emperor there at the Rajasuya.

questions.q: Through what did the Pandavas lose Indraprastha to the Kauravas?
options: The game of dice at Hastinapura
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira staked and lost the kingdom of Indraprastha to Shakuni's dice in the game at Hastinapura.

questions.q: Why did Dhritarashtra divide the kingdom and send the Pandavas to Khandavaprastha?
options: To defuse the succession feud once the Pandavas returned alive with Panchala's alliance
Gandhari begged it of him as a boon
The gods decreed it through the sage Narada
questions.explanation: After the Pandavas survived the lac house and won Drupada's alliance through Draupadi, partition seemed the only way to avert open conflict.

questions.q: How long did Maya labor to complete the great hall at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: The Sabha Parva says Maya took fourteen months to finish the unrivalled assembly hall.

questions.q: Why did Maya offer to build a wondrous hall for the Pandavas?
options: Gratitude for his life spared at the Khandava burning
Payment for sanctuary from Indra's wrath
Ransom for his captured danava kin
A debt owed to Yudhishthira's father Dharma
questions.explanation: Spared from the flames of Khandava at Arjuna's word, Maya insisted on repaying the debt with his craft.

questions.q: Before the Rajasuya at Indraprastha, which direction did Arjuna subdue?
questions.explanation: In the conquest of the four quarters, Arjuna marched north while his brothers took the other directions.

questions.q: Who was struck down at Indraprastha's Rajasuya for insulting Krishna?
questions.explanation: After enduring a hundred insults, Krishna loosed the Sudarshana and beheaded Shishupala at the hundred-and-first.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana's drenching in the pool at Indraprastha help set in motion?
options: The invitation to the dice game at Hastinapura
The building of the house of lac
Karna's coronation as king of Anga
questions.explanation: Humiliated and burning with envy, Duryodhana returned home and plotted with Shakuni the dice game that would strip the Pandavas of everything.

questions.q: According to the epic, who laughed when Duryodhana stumbled in the hall of illusions?
options: Bhima and Arjuna with the twins and attendants
Krishna, who then begged his pardon
questions.explanation: Vyasa's text names Bhima, Arjuna, the twins, and the servants as the laughers, while Draupadi's famous taunt about the blind king's son belongs to later retellings.

questions.q: What double illusion undid Duryodhana in Maya's hall?
options: Mirrors that doubled the hall's pillars
A ceiling painted as open sky
Stairs that seemed to climb yet descended
Dry crystal he took for water, then a real pool he took for crystal
questions.explanation: Duryodhana hitched up his robes crossing solid crystal he thought was water, then fell soaked into a true pool he thought was floor.

questions.q: To whom was the first honor offered at the Rajasuya of Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: On Bhishma's counsel the agra-puja, the first worship, was offered to Krishna, which provoked Shishupala's outburst.

questions.q: Which emperor had to fall before the Rajasuya at Indraprastha could proceed?
questions.explanation: Jarasandha, who held scores of kings captive, blocked any claim to empire, so Bhima slew him in a wrestling match before the sacrifice.

questions.q: Who finally received Indraprastha after the Pandavas departed on their last journey?
questions.explanation: Parikshit was crowned at Hastinapura while Vajra, Krishna's great-grandson, was established at Indraprastha.

questions.q: Indraprastha was also called Shakraprastha; who is Shakra?
questions.explanation: Shakra is a common name of Indra, so Shakraprastha carries the same meaning as Indraprastha.

questions.q: In popular retellings, which architect raised the city of Indraprastha itself?
options: Nala, who bridged the sea for Rama
Ribhu, smith of the Vedic gods
questions.explanation: Later retellings credit Vishvakarma with building the city at Krishna's request, while the epic itself describes the founding rites being led by Vyasa.

questions.q: Which site in Delhi does tradition most closely link with Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: The Purana Qila mound is traditionally identified as the site of Indraprastha, and a village called Indarpat stood there into modern times.

questions.q: From which mountain lake did Maya fetch treasures, including the conch Devadatta, to furnish the hall?
questions.explanation: The Sabha Parva says Maya brought riches, a mighty club for Bhima, and the conch Devadatta for Arjuna from Lake Bindusara near Kailasa.

questions.q: What status did the completed Rajasuya confer on Yudhishthira?
options: Samrat, sovereign over the kings of the earth
questions.explanation: The Rajasuya consecrated Yudhishthira as samrat, the emperor acknowledged by the assembled kings.

questions.q: Back in Hastinapura, who turned Duryodhana's envy of Indraprastha into a working plan?
options: Shakuni, proposing to win it all with dice
Dushasana, plotting a second lac house
questions.explanation: Shakuni assured Duryodhana that his unbeatable dice could win what armies could not, and the invitation to play was sent.

questions.q: What does the element prastha in the name Indraprastha mean?
questions.explanation: Prastha denotes a level expanse of land, so Indraprastha is the plain of Indra.

questions.q: Besides the deceptive pool, what else fooled Duryodhana in the hall of illusions?
options: A stair that ended at a painted wall
A garden of jeweled trees he took for real
Crystal doors, striking one that seemed open and shrinking from one that was
questions.explanation: Duryodhana struck his brow on a closed crystal door that looked open and then hesitated before a truly open one, compounding his humiliation.

questions.q: By Duryodhana's own admission to Dhritarashtra, what galled him most at Indraprastha?
options: Bhima's appetite at the royal feasts
The world's kings streaming tribute to Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: Duryodhana confessed that the wealth and homage pouring in from every king at the Rajasuya consumed him with envy, while Draupadi's jibe is a later embellishment.
